rationale.R1.2: Multiple load-bearing consistency failures: (1) The Results section reports ResAdapt residual 4.7e-2 vs baseline 8.1e-3 — ResAdapt is ~6× WORSE by this metric, yet the prose declares it 'better'; the conclusion is the logical inverse of the numbers. (2) The theoretical motivation invokes a 'deep ReLU ansatz' while the Setup section specifies a tanh MLP — the cited theoretical guarantees do not apply to the actual architecture. (3) The Discussion then treats the (inverted) gap as confirming the O(N^{-2}) prediction, compounding the first error.
rationale.R1.3: The central empirical claim — 'direct evidence that ResAdapt better satisfies the underlying Burgers' PDE' — is directly contradicted by the reported numbers: 4.7e-2 > 8.1e-3, meaning the adaptive scheme achieves a larger (worse) mean PDE residual than the uniform baseline. No re-reading makes this consistent with the stated interpretation.
rationale.R1.7: Hassanpour & Mehta (1987) is cited for Theorem 4.1 establishing convergence rates for a 'deep ReLU ansatz on bounded Lipschitz domains.' Deep ReLU networks in the contemporary ML sense did not exist as a studied object in 1987; the citation is either fabricated, severely mislabeled, or attributed to the wrong year/authors. This is a load-bearing reference: the entire theoretical motivation section rests on it.
